Off Calendar Oct-06-2011 Continued To Oct-11-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 502
MINI MINUTES: ***Group 1 Case, Lead Case 275332-Verworn***

Judge James Robertson, Clerk Karissa Shaw. (502)
Reporter: Sheila Arceno, CSR #13293 (Hired for Utterback, 275121)
Sarnoff Court Reporters, (415) 274-9977

APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff's counsel Chris Hersom and Jennifer Alesio of Brayton Purcell LLP.
(Verworn) Rich-Tex counsel Michael J. Boland of Imai Tadlock.
Douglass Insulation counsel Rhonda Woo of Selman Breitman.
(Thompson) NACCO MHG Inc. counsel Elizabeth R. Bain of Foley & Mansfield.
Thomas Dee Engineering Co. counsel Dana Burch of Walsworth Franklin.

At 2:30 p.m., counsel informs the court that Union Carbide has settled out of the Walker case. The court consolidates the Walker case with Group 1 for trial.

The plaintiff requests that the Utterback case be consolidated as well because the cancer allegation is being dropped from the complaint. After hearing argument, the court takes the matter under submission.

Pertinent pre-hardship motions in limine for the cases are discussed by court and counsel. The court will decide whether or not to consolidate Utterback on Tuesday morning (10/11/11). Jury hardshipping will be conducted that day in the afternoon.

The court takes a recess from 3:11 p.m. to 3:15 p.m.

The court will need a generic statement of the case from counsel on Tuesday morning. Counsel will not need to be present for jury hardshipping. The motion to consolidate Utterback will be served on defendants in Groups 1 and 2 as well as the Utterback defense counsel.

The cases will reconvene on Tuesday, October 11, 2011 at 10:00 a.m. for pre-hardship motions in limine, the consolidation ruling on Utterback and the generic statement of the case. Jury hardshipping will commence on that day at 1:30 p.m. Voir dire will take place on Wednesday, 10/12/11, at 9:30 a.m.

At 3:30 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-05-2011 Continued To Oct-06-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 502
MINI MINUTES: Judge James Robertson, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (502)

APPEARANCES:
Plaintiffs' counsel Chris Hersom of Brayton Purcell.
Bryan Steam LLC counsel Madeline Buty of Buty & Curliano.

At 3:57 p.m., the court takes the bench and the proceedings commence. Counsel have elected to not hire a court reporter. Therefore, the sign-in sheets produced by the courtroom clerk will list the appearances in this matter. (The mini-minutes in IJS will reflect what's on the sign-in sheets.)

The Cronin (274286) and Gleason (275034) cases are 'stand alone' cases and will not be consolidated. There has been no motion for consolidation in the Utterback (275121) matter. Counsel dispute whether there is a pending settlement with defendant Union Carbide in the Walker case (274978). The court will not consolidate the Walker case until official confirmation is received on the settlement.

At 3:56 p.m., the court hears argument on the motion to consolidate.

At 4:03 p.m., the court makes its rulings;

Group 1: Verworn (275332), Thompson (274573), Tracy (275123), Daggs (275457) and Hoover (275131).

Group 2: Daily (275437), Harrah (275456), Sharp (275011), Sherfy (275405), Tiburcio (275415) and Veio (275443).

Group 1 will go to trial first, followed by Group 2, Gleason, Cronin and then Utterback (unless it's consolidated).

Motions in limine (in all cases) that must be ruled on before jury selection will be heard tomorrow (10/6/11) at 2:00 p.m. Jury hardshipping for Groups 1 and 2 will be conducted next Tuesday (10/11/11). Unless counsel can produce a generic juror questionnaire for hardshipping, then no questionnaire will be used at all.

Court and counsel confer on the Gleason matter. According to counsel, all motions in limine to be heard before jury selection have been ruled upon by Judge Jackson. The court acknowledges this status, but still requires counsel to appear tomorrow for the hearing.

At 4:15 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emilie E. De La Motte, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Oakfabco, Inc.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Art Hoover

RULING:
DEFENDANT OAKFABCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate in its separate statement that defendant propounded interrogatories on plaintiff seeking all facts, witnesses and documents in support of plaintiff's claims against it and by failing to demonstrate that plaintiff's responses thereto were factually devoid, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Further, purported undisputed fact no. 4 states that plaintiff only identified himself and Mr. Hagele as witnesses as to defendant, and cites to its special interrogatory no. 1. However, defendant's interrogatory no. 1 did not ask for the identity of persons with knowledge of facts supporting plaintiff's claims against defendant and plaintiff's response to no. 1 identified two co-workers (Mr. Cuddihy and Mr. Chacon) who are not mentioned in defendant's separate statement. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, oppression or fraud to warrant punitive damages. Defendant's separate statement does not even mention this issue. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Summer Smith, Esq. of MURPHY PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY (415-788-1900), appearing for defendant / moving party Industry Products Company
Oren Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Art Hoover, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT INDUSTRY PRODUCTS COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Industry Products Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant, that defendant made a false representation about the product, or that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. By failing to submit plaintiff's responses to specially-prepared contention discovery, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The mere fact, without more, that plaintiff testified at his deposition that he does not recall the name Industrial Products Company is insufficient to satisfy defendant's burden or production. Order signed in open court. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7707. =(220/HEK)
